findings.evidence: `rg -n "PUnit|punit_coact|instPUnit" TauCeti` only finds these new public declarations and the bodies of `ComoduleCat.zero`/`FGComoduleCat.zero`.
findings.fix: Make the `PUnit` comodule instance/proof private implementation detail and remove or privatize `punit_coact`; keep public access through `isZero_zero`, `hasZeroObject`, and the morphism lemmas.
findings.issue: The public declarations `Comodule.instPUnit` and `Comodule.punit_coact` expose the concrete `PUnit` implementation of the zero comodule, even though downstream code should use `ComoduleCat.zero`/`FGComoduleCat.zero` through their `IsZero` API.

findings.evidence: Lines 109, 115, 177, and 183 mark only the canonical `IsZero.to_`/`from_` maps as simp, while lines 101, 105, 168, and 172 define the arbitrary-morphism normal forms without `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the four named morphism-to-zero/morphism-from-zero lemmas; these rewrite only morphisms whose source or target is the named zero object and should not loop.
findings.issue: The characteristic normal forms `zero_hom_eq_zero`/`hom_zero_eq_zero` for `ComoduleCat.zero`, and the parallel `FGComoduleCat` lemmas at lines 168 and 172, are not marked `@[simp]`, so users cannot normalize maps to or from the named zero object without invoking the lemmas manually.

summary: The zero-object API for both bundled categories is now present, but the PR still exposes implementation details and leaves the main morphism normal forms out of `simp`. These are public-interface issues for the roadmap-facing zero object.
